description: Degree-preserving edge-rewiring null test. GAT trained on real directed lead-lag graphs vs graphs whose edge destinations are permuted (preserving edge count, source out-degrees, and edge attributes). Model seed 42; LOCO all_7; 24h horizon. Tests whether GAT's gain is from the genuine topology or merely from having edges.

SVB_2023.interpretation: Rewiring collapses GAT to the base rate (0.293); real GAT (0.331) sits above the rewired distribution. The directed topology, not edge count, carries the signal.

FTX_2022.interpretation: Rewiring collapses GAT to ~base rate; topology carries the signal.

Terra_2022.interpretation: Rewiring does NOT degrade Terra (rewired >= real), consistent with Terra being the weak, least-topology-driven fold reported elsewhere in the paper.
